member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.AutofacPooledObjectPolicy`1
member.summary: Provides the needed for creating/returning objects in the pool in an Autofac way.
member.typeparam: The type of object being pooled.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.AutofacPooledObjectPolicy`1.#ctor(Autofac.Core.Service,Autofac.ILifetimeScope,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The pooled instance service used to resolve a new instance of the pooled items.
The owning scope of the pool.
The registration policy for the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.DefaultPooledRegistrationPolicy`1
member.summary: Provides the default .
member.typeparam: The type of object being pooled.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.DefaultPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.DefaultPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The maximum number of instances that should be retained in the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledComponent
member.summary: Components implementing this interface are aware that they are pooled, so will be notified when they are retrieved from the pool, and when they are returned to the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledComponent.OnGetFromPool(Autofac.IComponentContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Autofac.Core.Parameter})
member.summary: Invoked when this instance is retrieved from the pool. Any dependencies retrieved using the provided MUST be discarded in , otherwise memory leaks may occur.
member.param: The component context for the current resolve request.
The parameters to the resolve request.

summary.para: Invoked when this instance is being returned to the pool. Any dependencies retrieved in should be discarded in this method.

If the maximum pool capacity has been reached (or a custom decides not to return the instance to the pool), this method will be invoked, but then the object will not be put back in the pool (and may be disposed if the component implements ).

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1
member.summary: Defines the interface for a custom pooled registration policy, that defines what happens when an instance is retrieved from the pool, and what happens when the instance is returned to the pool.
member.typeparam: The type of object being pooled.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.MaximumRetained
member.summary: Gets a value indicating the maximum number of items that will be retained in the pool. This is not a limit on the number of objects that can be created, it's the threshold past which any objects returned to the pool will be discarded.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.Get(Autofac.IComponentContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Autofac.Core.Parameter},System.Func{`0})
member.summary: Invoked when an instance of is requested. The policy can invoke to retrieve an instance from the pool. Equally, it could decide to ignore the pool, and just return a custom instance.
member.param: The current component context.
The set of parameters for the resolve request accessing the pool.
A callback that will retrieve an item from the underlying pool of objects.
member.returns: An instance of from the pool or a custom instance.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.Return(`0)
member.summary: Invoked when an object is about to be returned into the pool. This method should be used to clean up the state of the object after being used (resetting connections, releasing temporary resources, etc).
member.param: The pooled object.
member.returns: True if the object should be returned to the pool. False if it should not be placed back in the pool (and will be disposed immediately if it implements ).

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolActivator`1
member.summary: An activator that creates the backing a pooled registration.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the objects in the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PoolActivator`1.#ctor(Autofac.Core.Service,System.Func{Autofac.IComponentContext,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{`0}},System.Func{Autofac.IComponentContext,Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.ObjectPoolProvider})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The service used to resolve new instances of the pooled registration.
A factory that returns the policy to use, invoked when the pool is built.
An optional factory that returns the that creates the backing pool, invoked when the pool is built. When , the default is used, sized from .
member.remarks: Both factories are invoked once during resolve, so the is available for resolving dependencies.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceContext`1
member.summary: Associates a resolved object pool with the policy used to build it.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the objects in the pool.
member.remarks: The policy is created when the pool is built and shared with the get-side activator through this wrapper, so both sides use the same policy instance.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceContext`1.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.ObjectPool{`0},Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The object pool.
The resolved registration policy.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceContext`1.Pool
member.summary: Gets the object pool.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceContext`1.Policy
member.summary: Gets the resolved registration policy, shared between pool creation and retrieval.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceContext`1.Dispose
member.summary: Releases the resources used by the wrapper.
member.remarks: The container owns this wrapper through the root scope and disposes it at shutdown. Disposal cascades to when the pool implements , so the pool can release any instances it has retained.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1
member.summary: Tracking class that that will return the wrapped instance to the provided pool when it is disposed.
member.typeparam: The type of the object being pooled.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.ObjectPool{`0},`0)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The pool.
The instance to wrap.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1.Instance
member.summary: Gets the wrapped instance.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the container, returning the instance to the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceUnpackMiddleware`1
member.summary: Middleware for extracting the requested instance from the pool instance container.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the registration that has been pooled.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledLifetime
member.summary: Lifetime wrapper to help us detect if we finished registering in a pooled configuration.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivator`1
member.summary: Activator that resolves the object pool and retrieves from that rather than creating a new instance.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the objects in the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivator`1.#ctor(Autofac.Pooling.PoolService)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The service used to access the pool.
member.remarks: The pool and its policy are retrieved from the resolved through at resolve time.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolService
member.summary: Defines a service used to access an , ensuring that each pooled item registration gets a different pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PoolService.#ctor(Autofac.Core.IComponentRegistration)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The registration for the pooled item.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions
member.summary: Provides registration methods for setting up pooled instances.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2})
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single shares one instance taken from a pool, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.

remarks.para: The pool retains up to twice the processor count ( x 2) instances. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.
To run behavior when an instance is taken from or returned to the pool, implement on the component, or use an overload that accepts a custom .

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Int32)
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single shares one instance taken from a pool, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
The maximum number of instances to retain in the pool.

remarks.para: The pool retains up to instances. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.
To run behavior when an instance is taken from or returned to the pool, implement on the component, or use an overload that accepts a custom .

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{``0})
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single shares one instance taken from a pool governed by a custom policy, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A custom policy that controls pool behavior.

remarks.para: The policy gives fine-grained control over how instances are retrieved from the pool, including whether an instance is returned to the pool at all.

The pool retains up to
instances. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Func{Autofac.IComponentContext,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{``0}})
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single shares one instance taken from a pool governed by a policy from the supplied factory, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A factory that returns the policy to use, invoked when the pool is built.

remarks.para: The factory is invoked with the current , so the policy can be resolved as a component and have its dependencies managed by the container (for example, ctx => ctx.Resolve<IMyPolicy>()).
The pool retains up to the value returned by the factory. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Func{Autofac.IComponentContext,Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.ObjectPoolProvider})
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single shares one instance taken from a pool whose storage and eviction are controlled by a custom , returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A factory that returns the that creates the backing pool, invoked once when the pool is built.

remarks.para: The factory is invoked once, resolved from the pool-owning (root) scope, so it can resolve dependencies from the (for example, ctx => ctx.Resolve<ObjectPoolProvider>()). Autofac still owns construction of the pooled instances and the pooling callbacks; the provider only controls where instances are stored and when they are evicted.
Because the provider owns sizing and eviction, does not size the pool. The pool must be thread-safe, because it is shared across all lifetime scopes and threads.

If the pool implements , the container disposes it at shutdown. Because reports no result and there is no eviction callback, the pool is responsible for disposing instances it declines on return or evicts asynchronously.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.

member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single shares one instance taken from a pool whose behavior is controlled by a custom policy and whose storage and eviction are controlled by a custom , returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A factory that returns the policy to use, invoked when the pool is built.
A factory that returns the that creates the backing pool, invoked once when the pool is built.

remarks.para: Both factories are invoked once, resolved from the pool-owning (root) scope, so they can resolve dependencies from the . The policy controls how instances are retrieved from and returned to the pool; the provider controls where instances are stored and when they are evicted. Autofac still owns construction of the pooled instances and the pooling callbacks.
Because the provider owns sizing and eviction, does not size the pool. The pool must be thread-safe, because it is shared across all lifetime scopes and threads.

If the pool implements , the container disposes it at shutdown. Because reports no result and there is no eviction callback, the pool is responsible for disposing instances it declines on return or evicts asynchronously.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Object[])
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of these tags shares one instance taken from a pool, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
The tags identifying the matching lifetime scopes.

remarks.para: Dependent components in scopes nested below a matching scope share that scope's instance.
The pool retains up to twice the processor count ( x 2) instances. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.
To run behavior when an instance is taken from or returned to the pool, implement on the component, or use an overload that accepts a custom .

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.
member.exception: Thrown at resolve time when no scope tagged with one of exists in the hierarchy.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Int32,System.Object[])
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of these tags shares one instance taken from a pool, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
The maximum number of instances to retain in the pool.
The tags identifying the matching lifetime scopes.

remarks.para: Dependent components in scopes nested below a matching scope share that scope's instance.
The pool retains up to instances. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.
To run behavior when an instance is taken from or returned to the pool, implement on the component, or use an overload that accepts a custom .

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.
member.exception: Thrown at resolve time when no scope tagged with one of exists in the hierarchy.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{``0},System.Object[])
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of these tags shares one instance taken from a pool governed by a custom policy, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A custom policy that controls pool behavior.
The tags identifying the matching lifetime scopes.

remarks.para: Dependent components in scopes nested below a matching scope share that scope's instance. The policy gives fine-grained control over how instances are retrieved from the pool, including whether an instance is returned to the pool at all.

The pool retains up to
instances. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.
member.exception: Thrown at resolve time when no scope tagged with one of exists in the hierarchy.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Func{Autofac.IComponentContext,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{``0}},System.Object[])
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of these tags shares one instance taken from a pool governed by a policy from the supplied factory, returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A factory that returns the policy to use, invoked when the pool is built.
The tags identifying the matching lifetime scopes.

remarks.para: Dependent components in scopes nested below a matching scope share that scope's instance. The factory is invoked with the current , so the policy can be resolved as a component and have its dependencies managed by the container (for example, ctx => ctx.Resolve<IMyPolicy>()).
The pool retains up to the value returned by the factory. Instances requested beyond that are disposed or discarded instead of being retained.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.
member.exception: Thrown at resolve time when no scope tagged with one of exists in the hierarchy.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Func{Autofac.IComponentContext,Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.ObjectPoolProvider},System.Object[])
member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of these tags shares one instance taken from a pool whose storage and eviction are controlled by a custom , returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A factory that returns the that creates the backing pool, invoked once when the pool is built.
The tags identifying the matching lifetime scopes.

remarks.para: Dependent components in scopes nested below a matching scope share that scope's instance. The factory is invoked once, resolved from the pool-owning (root) scope. Autofac still owns construction of the pooled instances and the pooling callbacks; the provider only controls where instances are stored and when they are evicted.
Because the provider owns sizing and eviction, does not size the pool. The pool must be thread-safe, because it is shared across all lifetime scopes and threads.

If the pool implements , the container disposes it at shutdown. Because reports no result and there is no eviction callback, the pool is responsible for disposing instances it declines on return or evicts asynchronously.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.
member.exception: Thrown at resolve time when no scope tagged with one of exists in the hierarchy.

member.summary: Configures the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of these tags shares one instance taken from a pool whose behavior is controlled by a custom policy and whose storage and eviction are controlled by a custom , returning it to the pool when the scope ends.
member.param: The registration to configure.
A factory that returns the policy to use, invoked when the pool is built.
A factory that returns the that creates the backing pool, invoked once when the pool is built.
The tags identifying the matching lifetime scopes.

remarks.para: Dependent components in scopes nested below a matching scope share that scope's instance. Both factories are invoked once, resolved from the pool-owning (root) scope. The policy controls how instances are retrieved from and returned to the pool; the provider controls where instances are stored and when they are evicted. Autofac still owns construction of the pooled instances and the pooling callbacks.
Because the provider owns sizing and eviction, does not size the pool. The pool must be thread-safe, because it is shared across all lifetime scopes and threads.

If the pool implements , the container disposes it at shutdown. Because reports no result and there is no eviction callback, the pool is responsible for disposing instances it declines on return or evicts asynchronously.

member.returns: The registration builder, to enable further configuration.
member.exception: Thrown at resolve time when no scope tagged with one of exists in the hierarchy.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.DefaultPolicyFactory`1
member.summary: Holds a cached default policy factory per closed , so the provider-only overloads do not allocate a new delegate per call.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc. This class was generated by MSBuild using the GenerateResource task. To add or remove a member, edit your .resx file then rerun MSBuild.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources.PolicyMustReturnInstance
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to The custom pool policy provided by {0} did not return an instance of {1} when requested..

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc. This class was generated by MSBuild using the GenerateResource task. To add or remove a member, edit your .resx file then rerun MSBuild.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources.Description
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Pool of {0}.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc. This class was generated by MSBuild using the GenerateResource task. To add or remove a member, edit your .resx file then rerun MSBuild.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.CannotUseProvidedInstances
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Pooled registrations cannot be used with provided instances. Use a typed registration or a delegate registration instead..

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.OnReleaseNotSupported
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to It is not possible to use custom OnRelease events for pooled registrations. Instead, either provide a custom IPooledRegistrationPolicy to handle items being returned to pool, or implement IPooledComponent on the component's type..

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.RequiresCallbackContainer
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to You can only specified a pooled registration for registrations that have a callback container attached (e.g., one that was made with a standard ContainerBuilder extension method)..
